chapter: Chapter: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: Why is matter considered the basic substance of the physical world?
questions.answer: Because all physical objects are made of matter.

questions.question: Why are mixtures easier to separate than compounds?
questions.answer: Because their components are physically combined, not chemically bonded.

questions.question: What property distinguishes homogeneous mixtures from heterogeneous mixtures?
Why is air considered a homogeneous mixture?
questions.answer: Because its gases are uniformly mixed.

questions.question: What chemical compound causes limewater to turn milky in the CO₂ test?
Why are pure substances difficult to separate by physical methods?
questions.answer: Because they consist of only one type of particle.

questions.question: Why are elements considered the simplest forms of matter?
questions.answer: Because they cannot be broken down into simpler substances chemically.

questions.question: What type of particle represents the smallest unit of an element?
Why do atoms combine to form molecules?
Which chemical process splits water into hydrogen and oxygen?
Which gas is released at the cathode during electrolysis of water?
Why do compounds have properties different from their elements?
questions.answer: Because chemical reactions create new substances with new properties.

questions.question: What compound forms when iron reacts chemically with sulfur?
Why can iron be separated from sulfur in a mixture using a magnet?
questions.answer: Because iron is magnetic while sulfur is not.

questions.question: Why cannot iron be separated from iron sulfide using a magnet?
questions.answer: Because iron sulfide is a compound with new properties.

questions.question: What type of mixture is formed when metals combine without chemical reaction?
Which alloy is commonly used to make utensils and musical instruments?
What natural substances form the basic components of rocks?
What characteristic must a substance have to be called a mineral?
questions.answer: It must be naturally occurring with fixed composition.

questions.question: Which physical method separates insoluble solids from liquids?
Which separation method relies on differences in boiling points?
Which process forms pure solid crystals from a solution?
What classification divides matter into elements, compounds, and mixtures?
Why are chemical reactions important in forming compounds?
questions.answer: They rearrange atoms to create new substances.

questions.question: Why is understanding matter important for science and technology?
questions.answer: It helps scientists design new materials and understand natural substances.
